volume: Foreign Relations of the United States, 1989-1992, Volume XIV, Arab-Israeli Dispute
policy: Direct sweep of Presidential Memcon and Telcon Files. Public site includes unrestricted item-level presidential conversations with online PDFs and visible MEPP topic signals in the catalog metadata.

eventWindows.label: Opening Phase
eventWindows.description: Early Bush administration contacts, Baker diplomacy, and pre-Gulf-crisis peace-process positioning.

eventWindows.label: Gulf War Linkage
eventWindows.description: Coalition diplomacy, Israeli restraint, and Arab-state positioning during the Kuwait crisis and war.

eventWindows.label: Road to Madrid
eventWindows.description: Postwar shuttle diplomacy, invitation formula debates, and regional preparations for a conference.

eventWindows.label: Madrid Conference
eventWindows.description: Madrid opening, bilateral meetings, and the U.S.-Soviet co-sponsored conference moment.

eventWindows.label: Post-Madrid Tracks
eventWindows.description: Bilateral rounds, loan guarantees, settlements, and multilateral-track follow-through.

eventWindows.label: Israeli Transition
eventWindows.description: Rabin government transition, late Bush administration diplomacy, and handoff issues.
